rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JOHN M. WEBB REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To Interrogatories And Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents; To Deem Admitted Truth Of Facts; For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Petitioner's Motion to Compel Responses to Form and Special Interrogatories; Responses to Request for Production of Documents; for RFA's to be deemed admitted; and for Sanctions is granted in part and denied in part. Per calculation under the April 30, 2020 General Order Re: Implementation of Emergency Orders of the time in which to respond to discovery, this motion was brought before the 30-day period to respond to discovery had run. No opposition to the motion was filed. There is no indication before the Court that response to the discovery at issue has been served prior to the hearing. Respondent is ordered to respond to the form and special interrogatories propounded on April 15, 2020, with objections waived and without use of the response option under CCP section 2030.230 (CCP section 2030.290) within 15 days of this order. Respondent is ordered to respond to the Request for Production of Documents propounded on April 15, 2020, with objections waived (CCP section 2031.300) within 15 days of this order. The Requests for Admission propounded on April 15, 2020 are deemed admitted (CCP section 2033.280). Petitioner's sanctions request is granted in part and denied in part. Respondent's failure to respond to the discovery propounded in any manner whatsoever warrants sanctions in the amount of $1,140. Respondent is ordered to pay sanctions in the amount of $1, 140 to Petitioner within 30 days of this order. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on October 28, 2020. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate.
